creator: joe@zircon.seattle.wa.us
text:
Whenever I run portupgrade to catch any upgraded ports, it 
	ALWAYS chooses to try to upgrade clutter, even though there 
	is absolutely no need to upgrade it.  And, because the upgrade 
	is unnecessary, then install ALWAYS fails because the clutter 
	is already installed, so the whole process FAILS due to the 
	failure of clutter.  I can see no reason to continually install 
	clutter and continually fail at it and cause everything to fail.  
	I cannot fix this problems, because if I use the -f switch 
	to get around the problem, then when I try to finish the 
	portupgrade the next time I run portupgrade it tries to 
	reinstall clutter and fails again.  There is no way to simply 
	finish the portupgrade.

Fix: 

??
How-To-Repeat: 	portupgrade -f --batch

	watch it fail trying to reinstall clutter and everything after 
	clutter fails.

attached listing of clutter log
